pattern: Each character contributes to the unique-count of exactly the substrings where it appears once, so summing per-character contributions from the gaps to its previous and next equal character gives the total.
hints: Instead of scoring substrings, score each position by how many substrings it is the sole occurrence of its letter in.
For a letter at index i, look at the nearest equal letter before it and after it; the substrings in which i is unique are bounded by those two neighbours.
The contribution of index i is (i - prev) times (next - i), where prev and next are the indices of the closest same letters (or the out-of-bounds sentinels); sum this over all positions.

edge_inputs.description: second example, a repeated letter reduces the count
all identical letters, every multi-letter substring loses uniqueness

stress.description: long random string over a small alphabet to force many repeats
stress.note: narrow alphabet packs repeats so the prev/next bookkeeping is exercised
